_meta.framework_note: Repo does not vendor CW SpecKit (.specify/templates missing). Tasks emitted directly under docs/plans/ per repo convention. Schema is a self-describing pragmatic JSON shape — task fields documented in tasks-template comments below.

fields.id: stable task id, T-P{phase}.{step} matching plan §6 substeps
fields.phase: p1 | p2 | p3 | p4 | p5 | verify
fields.description: single-sentence imperative — what this task does
fields.parallel: true if this task can run concurrently with siblings in the same phase (different target_file)
fields.target_file: absolute-from-repo-root path of the file edited or created — required when parallel=true
fields.depends_on: array of task ids that must be completed before this one
fields.exit_criteria: short string copied from the plan's validation column

critical_requirements.permissions: none — uses existing authService.getCurrentUserId() with 'default' fallback
high_risk_apis: streaming LLM via createStreamingLLM (unchanged signature; new system prompts)
critical_requirements.pre_checks: skip-schema (no DB changes); ui_changes triggers ui-preflight; pattern-check always runs

summary.phase_dependency_note: Strict linear dependency across phases per plan §6. Parallelism only safe within a single phase.
summary.deviation_note: Plan §9 anticipated '14 implementation + 1 QA' = 15. Tasks.json delivers 13 implementation + 1 QA + 2 pre-impl + 2 verify = 18 total. The 13 (vs 14 forecast) consolidates plan §9's P4.1–P4.5 into a single T-P4.MAIN_HEADER (one file, one PR slice) instead of five sub-tasks — preserves substep traceability via plan_ref.

tasks.description: Pre-implementation pattern compliance check (architectural patterns, naming conventions, IPC channel namespacing)
tasks.verify: Pattern check completes without FAIL status; warnings allowed
tasks.exit_criteria: Pattern check report Status: PASS or PASS-WITH-WARN

tasks.description: Pre-implementation UI preflight scan (header layout invariants, BrowserWindow lifecycle, hover-panel pattern audit)
tasks.verify: UI preflight scan completes; no critical blockers on header overflow risk (spec §11 T-15) or mode-picker positioning
tasks.exit_criteria: ui-preflight report generated; no BLOCKER findings

tasks.description: Add askMode='default' and preferredCodeLanguage='go' defaults to getDefaultSettings() in settingsService.js (after contentProtection: true)
tasks.exit_criteria: App boots; getSettings() returns both new keys for a fresh user

tasks.description: Append 3 new profile objects (pickle_glass_code, pickle_glass_debug, pickle_glass_system_design) to profilePrompts in promptTemplates.js — verbatim from spec §9.1, §9.2, §9.3
tasks.exit_criteria: Object.keys(profilePrompts) lists 'pickle_glass_analysis', 'interview', 'pickle_glass_code', 'pickle_glass_debug', 'pickle_glass_system_design'. {{PREFERRED_LANGUAGE}} token appears exactly once and only inside pickle_glass_code.content (P1.3 sanity-check)

tasks.description: Hoist `const settingsService = require('../settings/settingsService')` to top of askService.js (alongside other imports) and replace line 257's hard-coded 'pickle_glass_analysis' selection with mode-routing block per spec §7.3 (VALID_MODES coerce, PROFILE_BY_MODE map, language injection branch for code mode only)
tasks.exit_criteria: Manual edit of askMode to 'code' in electron-store + fire Ask → logs show pickle_glass_code profile selected; line 310 (multimodal-retry) still references the same outer systemPrompt variable

tasks.description: Add 6 new IPC handlers in featureBridge.js initialize(): mainHeader:getAskMode, mainHeader:setAskMode (with windowPool.get('header').webContents.send push of mainHeader:askModeChanged), mainHeader:openModePicker, modePicker:closeWindow, settings:getPreferredCodeLanguage, settings:setPreferredCodeLanguage
tasks.exit_criteria: Each invoke from preload returns expected value; setAskMode triggers webContents.send to header window

tasks.description: Modify existing ask:sendQuestionFromSummary handler (featureBridge.js line 83) per spec §7.4 — read askMode from settings; reject with {success:false, error:'Listen-summary actions only work in Default Ask mode…'} when askMode !== 'default'; otherwise call askService.sendMessage(userPrompt)
tasks.exit_criteria: With askMode='code' set in store, invoking summaryView.sendQuestionFromSummary('x') returns {success:false, error:...}; no LLM call made

tasks.description: Register mode-picker BrowserWindow in windowManager.js windowPool (mirror settings window registration at lines 526–557): width 160, height 144, frame:false, transparent:true, hasShadow:false, alwaysOnTop:true, skipTaskbar:true, show:false, resizable:false, focusable:true, parent:undefined. Load src/ui/modePicker/mode-picker.html. Add 'mode-picker' branch to handleWindowVisibilityRequest (mirror 'settings' block at lines 280–323). Introduce modePickerHideTimer parallel to settingsHideTimer with 200ms hide debounce. Add helpers showModePickerWindow / hideModePickerWindow / cancelHideModePickerWindow
tasks.exit_criteria: App boots; windowPool.has('mode-picker')===true; emitting internalBridge.emit('window:requestVisibility',{name:'mode-picker',visible:true}) shows the window at the right position

tasks.description: Add calculateModePickerWindowPosition() to windowLayoutManager.js mirroring calculateSettingsWindowPosition (lines 71–94). Anchor y = headerBounds.y + headerBounds.height + 5 (PAD=5); x = headerBounds.x + headerBounds.width - CARET_RIGHT_OFFSET - (modePickerBounds.width / 2), placeholder CARET_RIGHT_OFFSET=40px; clamp to display work area exactly as the settings position calculator does
tasks.exit_criteria: Window appears immediately below caret on a 1080p display; layout calc does not crash

tasks.description: Create new file src/ui/modePicker/mode-picker.html mirroring src/ui/app/header.html structure — script tag for the lit module, <mode-picker-view> custom element, glass styling block (backdrop-filter, dark translucent background, border-radius: 10px)
tasks.exit_criteria: HTML loads without 404 in renderer DevTools

tasks.description: Create new Lit element src/ui/modePicker/ModePickerView.js — properties: activeMode:string='default'; on connectedCallback fetch initial mode via window.api.mainHeader.getAskMode; render 4 rows (Default, Code, Debug, System Design) with ✓ on active; click handler MUST await setAskMode(mode) THEN await closeWindow() (ordering per clarify IMPL GAP 3 — no race); window.blur fallback close per spec §8.4
tasks.exit_criteria: Clicking each button writes to settings + closes window; Tab cycles focus; Enter activates focused row; blur fires closeWindow

tasks.description: Expose new IPC methods in preload.js: window.api.mainHeader.{getAskMode, setAskMode, openModePicker, cancelHideModePicker, onAskModeChanged, removeOnAskModeChanged}; new namespace window.api.modePicker.{selectMode, closeWindow}; on window.api.settingsView add {getPreferredCodeLanguage, setPreferredCodeLanguage}
tasks.exit_criteria: DevTools: window.api.mainHeader.setAskMode('code') returns success; window.api.modePicker.closeWindow() hides the window

tasks.description: Wire MainHeader.js — add askMode state property; connectedCallback fetches initial mode via IPC (try/catch with 'default' fallback per spec §8.3); add _renderAskLabel() returning 'Ask' / 'Ask · Code' / 'Ask · Debug' / 'Ask · SysDes'; add ▾ caret button after Ask pill inside .ask-group wrapper with mouseenter (cancelHideModePicker + openModePicker) and mouseleave (debounced hide) handlers; subscribe to mainHeader:askModeChanged push events in connectedCallback and unsubscribe in disconnectedCallback; CSS for .ask-group (flex, gap 4px) and .ask-mode-caret (14×14, -webkit-app-region: no-drag)
tasks.exit_criteria: Fresh load: badge shows 'Ask'; askMode='system_design' shows 'Ask · SysDes'; hovering caret opens picker; sliding mouse into picker before 200ms keeps it open; changing mode in picker updates badge instantly; caret clickable not draggable

tasks.description: SettingsView.js — add preferredCodeLanguage:{type:String,state:true} property; loadInitialData() reads via window.api.settingsView.getPreferredCodeLanguage(); add free-text input field in render() per spec §7.5 (Gemini model-ID style with @blur=handleSavePreferredLanguage); handleSavePreferredLanguage(value) calls window.api.settingsView.setPreferredCodeLanguage(value.trim())
tasks.exit_criteria: Opening Settings shows field with 'go' default; changing to 'python' and blurring saves; reopening shows 'python'

tasks.description: SummaryView.handleRequestClick (line 406) — surface result.error to user via alert() when result.success===false; wrap in try/catch with alert('Failed to send question. See logs.') on throw
tasks.exit_criteria: With askMode='code', clicking summary action shows native alert with spec's error string; no LLM call made

tasks.description: Execute the full manual smoke test plan from spec §11 (T1–T14) plus clarify-added T-15 in the order specified in plan §10 (T1 backward-compat → T2 Code default → T3 Code language change → T4 Debug 5-heading → T5/T5b SysDes routing A and B → T6 persistence → T7 Listen summary unchanged → T8/T9 summary follow-up default vs non-default REGRESSION GATE → T10 corrupted askMode coerce → T11 empty screenshot fallback → T12 picker dismiss on blur → T13 picker focus/Tab/Enter → T14 settings IPC failure on mount → T-15 header width visual gate). Tune CARET_RIGHT_OFFSET in calculateModePickerWindowPosition based on measured caret pixel position. Verify VALID_MODES coerce on 'banana' input and __INFER_FROM_SCREENSHOT__ sentinel substitution
tasks.exit_criteria: All 15 manual tests pass; picker visually centered under caret on multiple display sizes; PR ready

tasks.description: Documentation reconciliation — identify and update affected docs (README §How It Works if Ask-mode behavior surfaces there; CHANGELOG entry; in-app onboarding text if applicable)
Composite final gate — orchestrates all verification checks before merge. Conditional checks evaluated against this spec's critical_requirements.

composed_of.note: Lint is best-effort; repo does not currently enforce ESLint on the renderer Lit files. Existing repo conventions only.

composed_of.skip_reason: Repo is vanilla JS, no TS / no tsc step. Per plan §2 technical context.

composed_of.command: pattern-check on touched files (windowManager mirror-of-settings pattern, IPC namespacing, lit-element 5-slot prompt shape)

composed_of.skip_reason: Per plan §10 — repo has no renderer test harness wired for Lit elements; adding one is out of scope. Manual gate per spec §11 is the contract.
Same as unit — no IPC integration harness in repo.

composed_of.command: Run app, verify T1 backward-compat gate (Ask works identically to today in default mode)

composed_of.skip_reason: No DB schema changes (electron-store JSON only, no migration).

composed_of.command: Verify all 8 IPC channels listed in plan §7 are registered and invokable from DevTools per spec §7.7 inventory
Manual UI smoke — caret renders, picker opens/closes, badge updates live, settings field present
Execute spec §11 T1–T14 + T-15 manual test plan in plan §10 order

composed_of.skip_reason: ui_complexity=medium, no accessibility-specific keywords in spec. Picker focus/Tab/Enter covered by T13.

composed_of.command: Verify (a) {{PREFERRED_LANGUAGE}} injection sanitizes user input via .trim() per spec §8.2; (b) sentinel __INFER_FROM_SCREENSHOT__ is non-spoofable per spec §8.6 (only injected when field is empty); (c) askMode VALID_MODES coerce prevents profilePrompts.interview fallback per spec §8.1; (d) IPC handlers do not expose privileged main-process state to renderer beyond the documented contract

Review the diff for correctness — pattern-mirror fidelity vs settings window, race-condition in P3.5 button click ordering, MainHeader try/catch on IPC failure (spec §8.3), push event delivery via windowPool.get('header').webContents.send (NOT settings-updated broadcast — pre-existing dead-broadcast latent bug is out of scope per spec §12 and plan R4)
Run docs/plans/2026-05-25-ask-mode-shortcuts-acceptance-tests.yaml — all 15 manual tests + structural checks must pass

tasks.verify: All composed_of checks marked PASS or SKIP-WITH-REASON; no FAIL
tasks.block_on: Any check with blocks_merge=true marked FAIL

dependency_notes.linear_phase_gate: Plan §6 mandates: P1 → P2 → P3 → P4 → P5. Parallelization only safe within a single phase.
dependency_notes.P3.1_sequencing: T-P3.1 (windowManager) is NOT parallel with P3.3-P3.6 — windowManager edit must complete first because positioning code at P3.3 reads from windowPool registration done in P3.1.
dependency_notes.P4_independence: All three P4 tasks touch different files. They depend on prior phases but not on each other.
dependency_notes.QA_blocks_DOC_GATE: T-DOC-GATE cannot proceed until T-P5.QA passes — documentation reflects the final shipped behavior.
